nodes.label: Lit survey — the cheapest global-credit mechanism (mechanism-first deepening)
nodes.rationale: Supersedes track-c's survey-level treatment; the ~5-layer composing ceiling is a credit-reach (locality) limit, not an objective/task limit — asks what's the cheapest mechanism that delivers global credit without a deep backprop chain or a stream rewrite.

nodes.label: Three flow-safe ways to inject global credit
nodes.rationale: (1) change the local objective (InfoPro/CLAPP-style, always flow-safe); (2) broadcast a detached top-down signal driving a local weight update (DFA/EBD, flow-safe iff it drives an update not an activation rewrite); (3) widen the credit window (bounded backprop, w-family). The forbidden shape: inject a top-down value that becomes the next layer's activation (target-prop, PEPITA).

nodes.label: Family A — overlapping local windows (OLU / ContSup)
nodes.rationale: Bounded backprop of width k; ContSup forward-feeds detached earlier-block features into a later block's auxiliary head; the repo's own T0.2 already validated this (w2->L4, w4->L7); verdict: strongest cheap rung, we already own the apparatus, but bounded — extends ~5, doesn't reach 12.

nodes.label: Family B — DFA / EBD (global error broadcast)
nodes.rationale: Broadcast the top error through each layer's own fixed random matrix -> local Hebbian-style update, no chain; cheapest global-error option but canonically supervised and known to degrade on deep/conv tasks — no native contrastive error exists yet; keep as the global-error option but not first.

nodes.label: Family C — Forward Target Propagation (dead end)
nodes.rationale: Generates layer-wise targets via a second forward pass, no backward chain — but its core move is driving each layer's activation toward a target, which IS the forbidden stream-rewrite; verdict: dead end under the hard constraint despite being the most on-target named paper.

nodes.label: Family D — top-down feedback into local objective (CLAPP/GIM)
nodes.rationale: Keeps local InfoNCE, adds a detached top-down predictive/consistency term as a loss-term/modulator, never an activation replacement; the 2026 decisive result reaches BP-SSL parity by fixing the local update/objective, not by adding wiring — verdict: highest-leverage flow-safe family.

nodes.label: Family F — InfoPro / DGL / ContSup (preserve-downstream-info term)
nodes.rationale: Diagnoses greedy local collapse as discarding input info useless locally but needed downstream (= our density/drift diagnosis, published); fix = add an info-propagation term (a reconstruction loss) to the local objective — verdict: co-equal top pick with D, cheapest (no new wire, no extra forward pass, just a loss term), but must swap reconstruction for class-direction preservation (Phase-3 already rejected reconstruction).

nodes.label: Ranked shortlist — objective-side global credit first
nodes.rationale: 1. objective-side lambda_topdown consistency term (families D+F merged, cheapest, no new wire); 2. w4 with stride-2 overlap (family A, known-good baseline every global mechanism must beat); 3. DFA/EBD broadcast (family B, conditional, only if 1&2 plateau). FTP and PEPITA explicitly not worth implementing.
